summary: The case presents persistent grief with compulsive ex-social media checking that triggers transient somatic distress and negative self-evaluation. Modifiable levers span the social-ecological environment (digital boundary and social support), psychoeducational and experiential strategies (grief processing, cognitive-behavioral skills, mindfulness), and biological arousal regulation (sleep, somatic regulation). The proposed solutions aim to reduce cue-driven checking, mitigate distress and rumination, enhance supportive connectedness, and improve emotion regulation, using measurable, feasible, short-to-mid-term interventions across BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains and their interactions.

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital boundary management to reduce ex-social exposure
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a structured digital boundary protocol: unfollow/delete ex from feeds, mute notifications from ex-related content, set a daily social media check window (e.g., 2 x 10-minute periods), and use screen-time monitoring to enforce limits.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cue-induced craving and affective reactivity triggered by ex-content, lowers immediate physiological arousal, and weakens habit loop of checking.
solutions.measurement: Daily log of ex-social checks (times/day), urge intensity (0-10), nausea/somatic distress (0-10) during checks, and end-of-day mood rating.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C02, C04, C05, C06; Social boundary and digital-exposure reduction lever.

solutions.predictor_variable: Grief processing with acceptance and meaning-making (ACT-inspired)
solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver a brief ACT-based module focusing on accepting grief emotions, reducing experiential avoidance, clarifying personal values, and identifying committed actions aligned with social connection and self-worth.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Acceptance and values-driven action reduce avoidance and rumination, normalize grief, and improve self-appraisal through purposeful activity.
solutions.measurement: Grief intensity (ICG-Short Form or similar), daily mood, rumination frequency, and self-criticism ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C06, C03; ACT-based grief processing.

solutions.predictor_variable: Cognitive restructuring plus behavioral activation for self-evaluation
solutions.solution_candidate: Teach deliberate cognitive reframing to challenge automatic negative self-beliefs post-checking and to counter social comparison; pair with schedule-driven behavioral activation to build meaningful activities.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Alters neural and cognitive processing of self-evaluation and comparison; reduces rumination and reliance on checking as coping,
solutions.measurement: Self-criticism scale, rumination frequency (daily), activity engagement log.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C03, C06, C02; cognitive-behavioral restructuring with activation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Somatic distress regulation during distress episodes
solutions.solution_candidate: Teach diaphragmatic breathing, grounding, and brief autonomic stabilization techniques to mitigate nausea and autonomic arousal when exposed to triggering content.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Lower physiological arousal reduces immediate distress and the likelihood of turning to checking as a coping strategy.
solutions.measurement: Nausea/distress intensity (0-10) during distress episodes; frequency of checking during distress; self-reported relaxation before/after techniques.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C04; somatic regulation as coping with distress.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish regular sleep-wake times, limit screens 1 hour before bed, increase morning light exposure, and create a wind-down routine to stabilize mood and reduce rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep supports emotion regulation and reduces daytime reactivity to triggers, lowering rumination and distress.
solutions.measurement: Sleep diary (duration, quality), next-day mood, rumination frequency.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C04, C06; sleep optimization as biobehavioral lever.

solutions.solution_candidate: Facilitate regular contact with trusted friends/family, engage in brief social activities, and create a small supportive accountability network.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness buffers grief, reduces rumination, and provides external validation that diminishes self-criticism and checking impulses.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale, frequency of supportive interactions, mood ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C06, C02; social support enhancement.

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a daily short mindfulness practice (present-moment awareness, urge surfacing) to reduce reactivity to social media cues and self-criticism.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Mindfulness reduces automatic reactivity to distress and diminishes rumination and negative self-appraisal.
solutions.measurement: Mindfulness scale (e.g., FFMQ), momentary distress/urge logs, daily check-frequency.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01, C04, C06; mindfulness-based distress tolerance.
